articles.title: Mike Vrabel’s Hubris Made Him the Main Character of the NFL Draft - The Ringer
articles.description: The first round of the 2026 draft lacked juice. Enter the Patriots coach, NFL reporter Dianna Russini, and the sex scandal that won’t go away.

articles.title: Steelers thought they were getting Makai Lemon, didn’t realize Eagles had cut the line - NBC Sports
articles.description: As the Steelers moved quickly with the truncated window to make their first pick in the 2026 draft, they didn't know the guy they wanted was already gone.

articles.description: Although Christian Hubicki was voted out of Survivor following a twist in the game introduced by Jimmy Fallon, the castaway cleared the air with the comedian on The Tonight Show.

articles.description: Pope Leo XIV is urging the United States and Iran to return to talks to end the war. In a wide-ranging news conference Thursday on his way home from his trip to Africa, he also condemned capital punishment. Leo also asserted that countries have the right to c…

articles.title: Live Updates: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks following White House peace talks, Trump says - CBS News
articles.description: Following a second round of peace talks, President Trump announced that the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire had been extended by three weeks.

articles.title: Judge sets date for evidence against singer D4vd in killing of 14-year-old to be presented - PBS
articles.description: Lawyers for singer D4vd insisted Thursday on forcing prosecutors to show what evidence they have, and a judge set a hearing for next week for them to lay it out publicly in the killing and dismemberment of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

articles.title: Trump Says the US Is Weighing Purchase of Spirit Airlines - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: President Donald Trump said he is considering having the US purchase Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc., saying it could be a potentially good investment for the federal government.

articles.title: US Reduces Marijuana Restrictions in Lift to Ailing Industry - Bloomberg
articles.description: The US Justice Department reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous drug, a major shift that could increase legal cannabis sales and help a struggling industry.
